D5 Design L4 Analyze Reference

Turbo: a Fixed Rainbow

when you still want a rainbow, Turbo smooths jet's muddy bands and harsh jumps — though it's still not luminance-monotonic

Rainbows are bad for quantitative data but useful when sign and locality matter (depth maps, optical flow). Google's Turbo keeps the rainbow's familiarity while removing jet's worst sins: the muddy cyan and yellow bands and the abrupt lightness jumps. It is smoother — but its lightness still rises and falls, so it is not a substitute for viridis.

Compare Turbo against jet and their lightness curves. Turbo's curve is smoother and its colours cleaner, yet both rise then fall. For ordered data the monotonic viridis still wins.
